headline: Is Nvidia Stock a Buy Heading Into October? Warning Signs Flashing
summary: NVDA surged 56% over six months but slipped 2% in last 30 days. Q2 revenue hit $46.7B with gross margins over 72%. Warning signs include bubble-like capex levels and enterprises questioning AI ROI. Competitors eager to chip away at dominance.

headline: Tesla Q3 2025 Deliveries: 497,099 Vehicles, Up 7.4% YoY
summary: Tesla delivered 497,099 vehicles in Q3 2025, up 7.4% YoY. Revenue exceeded $28B, up 12%. Growth partly driven by US tax credit expiration pushing buyers to act before incentives ended. Strong quarter but questions about sustainability.

headline: Prediction: Nvidia Stock Will Soar Over the Next 10 Years - AI Spending Key Driver
summary: Analyst predicts NVDA shares near $189 will reach $400 by 2030. Forward P/E of 41.5 not far from 5-year average of 38.9. AI spending growth and data center dominance cited as key drivers. Nvidia invested in Intel and OpenAI.

headline: Scotiabank Reiterates Outperform on Alphabet, $310 Target on AI Chip Leadership
summary: Scotiabank maintained outperform rating with $310 target. Cited Alphabet's leadership in proprietary AI chips and search-monetization efficiency. Sell-side targets cluster between $190 and $315. Stock at $265 holding above all major moving averages.

headline: Alphabet-Anthropic Cloud Partnership Expands, Boosting AI Chip Demand
summary: Expanded cloud infrastructure partnership between Alphabet and Anthropic signals continued strong demand for AI compute. Positive for NVDA as primary GPU supplier. Market anticipating potential Fed rate cut and easing US-China trade tensions.

headline: Alphabet Q3 Earnings Preview: Approaching $100B Revenue Milestone, CapEx Focus
summary: Analysts predict Q3 revenue around $99.75-99.9B. Markets expect strong sales growth but profits held back by high investment spending. Key focus on advertising, cloud, and capital expenditure.

headline: Alphabet Tops $100 Billion Quarterly Revenue For First Time, Cloud Grows 34%
summary: Revenue $102.35B vs $99.89B estimated. EPS $3.10 adj vs $2.33 estimated. YouTube ad revenue $10.26B. Google Cloud revenue $15.15B, up 35% YoY. Cloud backlog reached $155B. Shares rose 5% in after-hours.

headline: Alphabet CEO: First-Ever $100 Billion Quarter, AI Driving Real Business Results
summary: Sundar Pichai: 'This was a terrific quarter. AI is now driving real business results across the company. Five years ago quarterly revenue was $50B, now doubled. Gemini processes 7 billion tokens per minute.' Double-digit growth across every major business segment.

headline: Alphabet Q3 FY 2025: Broad-Based AI-Driven Growth Across All Segments
summary: Google Services revenue $87.1B (+14% YoY). Search $56.6B (+15%). YouTube ads $10.3B (+15%). Cloud $15.2B (+34%). Capex outlook raised to $91-93B from ~$85B. 60% of Q3 capex went to servers. Cloud backlog of $155B provides strong forward visibility.

headline: NVIDIA Becomes First Company to Surpass $5 Trillion Market Cap
summary: NVDA hit all-time high of $212.19 on Oct 29, briefly pushing market cap above $5 trillion. Historic milestone making it the first publicly traded company to reach this level.

headline: Tesla Valued at 24x More Than Ford, Reflecting AI/Robotaxi Premium
summary: Tesla's massive valuation premium over legacy automakers reflects market pricing in autonomous driving and robotics potential. The difference shows Ford's perceived lack of future as much as Tesla's premium.

headline: DOJ Clears Alphabet's $32 Billion Wiz Acquisition After Antitrust Review
summary: DOJ cleared the antitrust review of Alphabet's $32B all-cash acquisition of Wiz, the cloud security startup. Largest acquisition in Alphabet history. Deal expected to close in 2026. Wiz brings agentless multicloud security capabilities to complement Google Cloud.

headline: Alphabet Completes Concurrent Bond Offerings of Dollar and Euro Senior Notes
summary: Alphabet completed concurrent underwritten public offerings of USD and EUR denominated senior notes. Multiple maturities from 2029 to 2054. Indicates strong balance sheet management and access to capital markets.

headline: NVIDIA Reports Record Q3 Revenue of $57.0 Billion, Up 62% Year-Over-Year
summary: Record Q3 FY2026 revenue of $57.0B, up 22% QoQ and 62% YoY. Data center revenue hit record $51.2B, up 66% YoY. CEO Jensen Huang: Blackwell sales off the charts, cloud GPUs sold out. GAAP EPS $1.30 beat expectations. Returned $37.0B to shareholders in first 9 months.

headline: NVIDIA Q3 Earnings Beat But Stock Begins Correction From October Highs
summary: Despite record earnings beating top and bottom lines, NVDA stock begins post-ATH correction. Stock had run up significantly into earnings. Some profit-taking despite strong results.

headline: Tesla at a Crossroads: Sales Slump in Europe Meets Regulatory Headwinds
summary: European registrations dropped 58% in France, 49% in Denmark. But Q3 global deliveries up 7.4% and revenue up 12%. US inventory of ~10,799 vehicles signals potential oversupply. Stock up despite sales headwinds.

headline: Tesla China Retail Sales Slip, Facing Impossible Task to Avoid First Annual Decline
summary: 73,145 retail sales in China in November, slight YoY decline from 73,490. YTD at 531,855, needing 125,000+ in December to avoid annual decline. Best month ever was 82,927. Competitors BYD up record growth, Xiaomi up 175%, Xpeng up 70%.

headline: Waymo Surpasses 14 Million Total Trips, Over 1 Million Monthly Rides
summary: Waymo reached 14 million total trips by December 10. Fleet of roughly 2,500 vehicles. Ambition to reach 1 million weekly rides by end of 2026. Expanding internationally including Tokyo and London plans.

headline: Tesla US Sales Drop to Under 40,000 Units, Lowest Since January 2022
summary: ~39,800 vehicles sold in US in November, 23% drop YoY. Lowest monthly volume since Jan 2022. Driven by federal EV tax credit expiration. However, Tesla market share actually increased to 56.7% as overall EV market fell 41%.

headline: Tesla's 2025 Was a Turning Point: Autonomy and Robotics Over Deliveries
summary: Tesla repositioned from automaker to AI platform company. Stock reacted more to FSD/autonomy milestones than delivery numbers. Launched limited geofenced robotaxi pilot in Austin with safety monitors. Optimus robot showed improvements but far from mass production.

headline: NVDA Analysts Project Significant Upside: Goldman $250, BofA $275, Cantor $300
summary: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley both target $250, Bank of America and Wedbush $275, Cantor Fitzgerald holds street-high $300. Projected 2026 revenue around $323B with $500B order backlog for Blackwell and Rubin chips.

headline: Waymo Testing Gemini AI Integration as In-Car Assistant in Robotaxis
summary: Researcher discovered 1,200+ line system prompt for unreleased Gemini integration in Waymo vehicles. Assistant can answer questions, manage climate control, and reassure riders. Waymo handling 450,000+ paid rides per week across six US cities.

headline: Tesla European Sales Plunge to Three-Year Low, Stock Defies Fundamentals
summary: EU/UK/EFTA registrations down 34.2% to 12,130 in November. YTD down 38.8%. Stock still up 20% YTD to ~$485 despite declining car sales. Market pricing in AI/self-driving/robotics narrative over auto fundamentals.

headline: Tesla: Prudent Buy on Pullbacks, Energy Segment Now Primary Profit Engine
summary: Energy segment driven by Megablock and AI data center demand becoming primary profit engine. $1.63T valuation justified by autonomy, energy, AI optionality. Watch auto gross margin >17%, energy deployment >12 GWh/quarter, FSD take rate >12%.

headline: Gemini 3 Model Launches, Alphabet Reports Dramatically Higher Engagement
summary: Gemini 3 model launched in December 2025. Management reported dramatically higher engagement. Serving unit costs dropped 78% over 2025 through model optimizations. Gemini app surpassed 750 million monthly active users, up from 400 million nine months earlier.

headline: Tesla Hit With Stunning Setback in China: First Annual Decline Looming
summary: November retail sales dropped YoY. Would need 125,000+ December deliveries to avoid first annual decline. Best month ever was 82,927 - making target mathematically near-impossible. Aging lineup with zero sign of next-gen models.

headline: Tesla: The Big Short of 2026 - Trading at 317x Earnings on Robotaxi Euphoria
summary: Bearish analysis: TSLA trading at 17.5x trailing sales and 317x earnings. Revenue expected to decelerate as Q3 tax credit sugar rush fades. Profitable regulatory credit income set to dry up as fuel economy fines eliminated.

headline: NVDA Down 9.42% From October ATH Despite Record Earnings, 60/64 Analysts Say Buy
summary: NVDA gained 2.71% over past 5 sessions but correction from Oct 29 ATH continues. Stock up 35.86% YTD. 60 of 64 analysts recommend buying, consensus target $253.19 suggesting 34% upside.

headline: Tesla Publishes Q4 2025 Delivery Consensus: 422,850 Vehicles, Full Year Down 8.3%
summary: Analyst consensus of 422,850 Q4 deliveries. Full-year 2025 consensus at 1,640,752 vehicles, 8.3% decline from 2024. Unusual transparency move to establish benchmark ahead of results.

headline: NVIDIA Soars 39% in 2025, Outperforming Semiconductor Industry
summary: NVDA gained 38.8% in 2025, outperforming semiconductor industry rise of 35.9%. Data center segment generated $51.22B in Q3, representing 89.8% of total sales. Forward P/E of 26.80x vs industry average 28.97x.
